commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper verdict: pass (sha=55ea1462c6455ffd7e5178742a201f8abd033ca8); PR is behind or has merge conflicts and needs a cloud rebase repair before automerge
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the devices CLI local fallback to approve a compatible same-device replacement request ID and adds focused CLI/infra coverage plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main throws a fallback mismatch when the gateway reports a newer requestId, while the pairing infra can supersede same-device pending requests during reconnect; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer-labeled PR with an auth-boundary behavior change should stay with maintainer review and current-main rebase rather than ClawSweeper repair automation.

target.title: fix(cli): recover replaced device approvals
target.body:
## Summary

- Recover `openclaw devices approve <requestId>` when a local same-device repair request supersedes the request being approved.
- Require the replacement to match the original device identity, role/client metadata, and a compatible scope superset before approving it via local fallback.
- Add focused infra and CLI tests for devices-first requestId churn, cron-first progressive authority, replacement approval, fail-closed cases, and JSON/interactive output.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/cli/devices-cli.test.ts src/infra/device-pairing-churn.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw devices approve <requestId>` no longer fails with an unclear `unknown requestId` when the same local CLI repair request has been superseded by a compatible replacement during the approval reconnect.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout on Linux after rebasing onto current `origin/main`; focused Vitest shards for CLI and infra pairing behavior.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/cli/devices-cli.test.ts src/infra/device-pairing-churn.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: Test output reported `src/infra/device-pairing-churn.test.ts (2 tests)` passed and `src/cli/devices-cli.test.ts (43 tests)` passed; `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD` produced no output.

Observed result after fix: same-device compatible replacement approvals use the replacement request ID, emit resolved metadata in JSON mode, print an interactive replacement notice, and fail closed for missing snapshots, incompatible scopes, cross-device replacements, public-key changes, role changes, metadata conflicts, remote `--url`, or missing pending requests.

What was not tested: Full packaged Docker/Crabbox smoke for the final branch; previous black-box validation was used to define the focused source-level regression coverage.

Refs: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/74484
target.author: hxy91819

commands.author: hxy91819
commands.repair_reason: Protected member PR with the maintainer label and no narrow repair finding; route through normal maintainer review and landing gates.; Cleared: The diff changes local LaunchAgent/env-file recovery and update env selection without new dependencies, network calls, permissions, or secret logging. (sha=7e59cdc50d53f788ac16ca3f7e1811aabdc08628)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates LaunchAgent service-env parsing/rewrite recovery and package update restart preparation so managed gateway service env is preserved during source/dev to package cutovers.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main reads post-update service state with `process.env` and only unwraps exact `-env-wrapper.sh` paths, while the PR adds tests for both failure modes. I did not run the tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Protected member PR with the `maintainer` label and no narrow repair finding; route through normal maintainer review and landing gates.
